title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape
summary: NASA has outlined updated strategies for Blue Origin and SpaceX to expedite the development of Artemis lunar landers. The revised plans aim to accelerate progress toward the Moon mission.

title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit (GEO) exposure. This move signifies a shift away from debt-fueled expansion in the company's satellite operations.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers warn that SpaceX's planned orbital data center satellites, set to launch as early as next year, could significantly interfere with astronomical observations. The concern highlights potential disruptions to scientific research due to the increasing presence of commercial space infrastructure.

title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp suggests he might be the last employee due to the company's focus on AI, following its involvement in the SPAC boom. The company is shifting its strategy to prioritize artificial intelligence.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, placing six smallsats into orbit. This marks its first mission since a failure in December.

title: In aerospace, AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s filling a shortage
summary: Aerospace executives view AI as a solution to labor shortages rather than a replacement for workers, addressing an overstretched industrial base. The industry is leveraging AI to enhance productivity amid workforce challenges.

title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a €10.9 million contract from ESA to develop and demonstrate VDES satellites for maritime monitoring. The project aims to enhance maritime communication and safety through a completed satellite constellation.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. This initiative aims to attract potential users for the new facility.

title: Understanding what’s next for orbital data centers
summary: SpaceNews discusses the future of orbital data centers in a recent event, exploring industry interest and technological advancements. Industry leaders and analysts share insights on the technology's potential.

title: Millions could see a rare sunset during the total solar eclipse on Aug. 12, 2026. Here's where to look
summary: A total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, will be visible to millions, offering a rare sunset-like experience. Observers should plan ahead to witness this unforgettable celestial event.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 214 — Moon Man
summary: The 214th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine about his past and the future of the space agency. The conversation covers his experiences and insights on NASA's direction.

title: How Japanese scientists sent a real-life Transformer to the moon
summary: Japanese scientists deployed an autonomous rolling rover that transformed from a sphere into a robot to explore the moon in 2024. This marks a significant advancement in lunar robotics and exploration technology.

title: Ever have a scary HR meeting on your calendar? That's how the Artemis 3 crew found out their assignments
summary: NASA used an unconventional method to inform the Artemis 3 crew of their assignments during a scheduled HR meeting. The crew found out about their roles in a surprising and unexpected way.

title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
summary: SpaceX launched 29 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket. The liftoff occurred shortly before the company went public on the NASDAQ stock market.

title: Japan's H3 rocket bounces back from failure with successful return to flight launch carrying 6 satellites
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight after a previous failure, carrying six satellites. The mission marks a recovery for the H3 program after a launch failure last year.

title: Could the secret to black hole formation be locked away in this record-breaking ancient quasar?
summary: A record-breaking ancient quasar from 12.9 billion years ago suggests that supermassive black holes formed rapidly in the early universe. This discovery could provide insights into black hole formation mechanisms.

title: Why is the US Space Force researching 'orbital warehouses'?
summary: The US Space Force is exploring 'orbital warehouses' to enhance space infrastructure in preparation for two 2027 missions testing spacecraft maneuvering. This initiative aims to address logistical challenges in future space operations.

title: 'Disclosure Day' review: A close encounter with Spielberg's brilliance that doesn't quite make contact
summary: A review of 'Disclosure Day' praises Spielberg's brilliance in sensational performances and cinematography but criticizes its predictable plot and lack of depth.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. SpaceX's Flight 12 was completed three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The launch marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. The milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will involve testing lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). The crew consists of veteran astronauts assigned to this historic lunar mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: China, Rocket Lab, and SpaceX (Starlink) are scheduled to conduct launches in the upcoming week from multiple locations. The flights include three Starlink missions and others from different regions.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent failure of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on future objectives despite the setback.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in advancing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This development marks a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and operational capabilities.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and developing materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for how space missions are conducted and communicated.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a spacecraft or satellite component designed for durability and precision.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: NASA conducted a successful test of the parachute system for the Mars Sample Return mission, simulating extreme conditions. This marks a critical step toward bringing Martian samples back to Earth.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its descent to the Red Planet in 2028.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO could significantly boost the entire space industry by attracting more investment. The move is expected to shift the industry's reliance on private capital toward public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the kind of economy that will emerge. The capital influx could either drive operational growth or merely lead to hardware accumulation.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a landmark event for the US space industry, with significant implications for Europe's space sector. The news highlights the global impact of SpaceX's financial milestone.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox launched an update to enhance AI agents' capabilities in analyzing geospatial data. The update aims to improve the effectiveness of AI-driven geospatial analytics.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO. The debate highlights differing opinions on the company's stock performance and the broader growth of the space industry.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. The Adagio-XL is designed to support data processing and storage in space.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital has raised $5 million in a pre-seed funding round to develop its ODC constellation. The ODC concept involves a network of smaller satellites designed to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for a 2027 Earth orbit mission as a test for future Moon landings. The Artemis III astronauts will meet these landers in orbit as part of the preparation.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has selected Spanish startup Arkadia Space to supply a propulsion system for its upcoming 2027 satellite mission. This collaboration highlights the growing role of European startups in advancing space technology.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX began public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price. This valuation made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is emerging as critical infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge, addressing the needs of enterprises managing remote assets. The integration enhances real-time monitoring and operational efficiency for industries like utilities and logistics.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to enhance international disaster preparedness and U.S. response efforts.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded three Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its satellite capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet as part of the PTS-G Swarm 1 program. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's involvement in U.S. military space initiatives.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, relying on investor confidence in Elon Musk and projected AI growth. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to go public amid high expectations.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has exited stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build a network focused on space transportation and infrastructure.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge has secured £10 million in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding will support the company's space manufacturing and technology development initiatives.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their cooperation in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The collaboration aims to enhance mutual security and technological advancements in the space sector.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation data company, is expanding into Brazil to leverage the country's climate pressures and rapid growth. The move marks a significant step in the company's international expansion.
